title: hordeum vulgare subsp. spontaneum
intro: Hordeum vulgare subsp. spontaneum, also known as wild einkorn, is a type of wild grass belonging to the Poaceae family. It is native to the Middle East and the Mediterranean region, and has been used for its medicinal properties for centuries. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. Hordeum vulgare subsp. spontaneum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional medicine to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
